semantics: Each realm installs a genuine default ReadableStream, ReadableStreamDefaultReader, and ReadableStreamDefaultController with hidden GC-traced state rather than treating arrays or iterables as streams.
Construction snapshots and validates the underlying start, pull, and cancel algorithms, observes the strategy high-water mark, and begins pulling only after start fulfillment.
Queued chunks and pending reads remain ordered; close drains an existing queue before done, while error rejects pending reads and the active reader's closed promise.
A stream exposes exact locked and disturbed transitions, supports direct and constructed default readers, rejects competing consumers, and releases the private consumer's reader after settlement.
Pull reentrancy is coalesced through pulling and pull-again state, and asynchronous pull rejection errors the stream without invoking user code under a native lock.
Cancellation clears the queue, fulfills pending reads as done, invokes the captured underlying cancel algorithm once, and adopts its return value or rejection.
Every private conversion returns an intrinsic realm-local Promise; non-stream brands throw synchronously while locked, stream-error, invalid-chunk, parse, and conversion failures reject.

ArrayBuffer and Uint8Array conversions preserve chunk order, check total-size overflow, reject detached views, snapshot views before later user code can mutate their backing stores, and fill the final destination without a temporary concatenation payload.

Text conversion incrementally-equivalent concatenates byte and string chunks, strips one leading UTF-8 BOM, preserves code points split across chunks, and applies Encoding Standard replacement semantics.
JSON conversion parses the completed realm-local text through that realm's JSON.parse and converts an abrupt parse into the returned Promise's rejection.
Blob conversion produces a realm-local byte-owning Blob; FormData supports both application/x-www-form-urlencoded and binary-safe multipart fields and File parts with boundary validation.
Same-VM sibling streams are consumable into the selected output realm, while foreign-VM handles fail synchronously and never expose their object graph.
All stream, reader, controller, queue, request, chunk, algorithm, and conversion-continuation edges are ordinary managed Values and therefore survive precise collection and relocation.
The Home and Bun declarations share identical encoded-value signatures, require no output ownership shim, and are audited against both exact pinned source revisions.
